rulings.Case Title: CALEF HOUSE, LLC, A BUSINESS ENTITY VS. FAY SERVICING, LLC, A BUSINESS ENTITY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday May 12, 2022, line 1. DEFENDANT CIVIC FINANCIAL SERVICES, LLC, FAY SERVICING, LLC, CIVIC VENTURES, LLC SOLELY AS ADMINISTRATOR AND DEMURRER to 4TH Amended COMPLAINT. Continued to August 1, 2022. Supplemental brief responsive to March 26, 2022 minute order to be filed no later than July 15, 2022. Supplemental brief filed on March 30, 2022 will not be considered in light of the accompanying declaration. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.= (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: OASIS GRILL, LLC ET AL VS. ERIC KETCHUM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Suheil Shatara
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday May 12, 2022, line 2. PLAINTIFF OASIS GRILL, LLC, MOHAMMAD ZUGHAIYER's Motion To Compel Deposition Of Suheil Shatara, GRANTED. Shatara shall appear for in-person deposition as a percipient witness on May 19, 2022 at 9:00 a.m. Cut off to hear any motion re: deposition is June 6, 2022 on a 5 day notice. Plaintiff may appear ex-parte to seek an order shortening time to set such motion. Sanctions granted as requested. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: CHARLES C. SCHELLER JR. ET AL VS. JAMES CHAFFEE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday May 12, 2022, line 4. 1-DEFENDANT JAMES CHAFFEE's DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT. Hearing Required. Plaintiff Charles C. Scheller, Jr to appear in person or via Zoom with video capability. First Amended Complaint was filed after ruling on the demurrer to the original complaint expressly instructing the Plaintiff "to name a plaintiff with standing and capacity to bring this action." Since Plaintiff does not provide any argument or authority that a deceased individual has capacity to sue and does not address the Death Certificate of which judicial notice was requested, the Court assumes that Charles C. Scheller, Jr, Plaintiff in this case, is alive. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.= (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION VS. KENNY CLAY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday May 12, 2022, line 5. DEFENDANT KENNY CLAY's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. DENIED. Defendant failed to meet his burden under CCP 473(b). Defendant was served by a registered process server, had experience with prior unlawful detainer actions in which he successfully sought help with responding to the summons and complaint as he understands the gravity of these matters. (Motion 2:8) Defendant failed to show mistake, surprise or excusable neglect for his failure to do so after having been served with the papers in this matter. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.= (501/CFH)

rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION TO PREVENT FORFEITURE DUE TO COVID-19 RENTAL DEBT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday May 12, 2022, line 6. DEFENDANT BRITTANY LEWIS APPLICATION TO PREVENT FORFEITURE DUE TO COVID-19 RENTAL DEBT. DENIED. Defendant failed to meet the requirements of CCP 1179.13 and provide evidence that there is a final decision approving rental assistance for the premises for the time period listed in the notice. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: KDF POST STREET, LP VS. EVERETT BUTT JR. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday May 12, 2022, line 7. PLAINTIFF KDF POST STREET, LP's Motion To Compel Discovery, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant. GRANTED, subject to opposition. Defendant to provide complete verified responses without objections to Special Interrogatories, Request for Production, Form Interrogatories General and Form Interrogatories UD no later than 2:00 p.m. on May 13, 2022 by hand-delivery or e-mail. Sanctions granted in the amount of $810. Plaintiff to prepare order and present it for execution at the hearing; Order to be personally served on Defendant no later than 2 p.m. on May 12, 2022. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
